notes: Cuts Vercel Fluid Active CPU on Hobby plan (was at 132% of the 14,400s monthly cap). Three changes: (1) amazon-sync schedule */5 -> */10 in gcp/setup.ps1 — 288 -> 144 invocations/day; (2) early-exit guard in amazon-sync route does a slim HEAD count(*) before loading AmazonSyncService + discordService (deferred via dynamic import), with a single-row noop record on the exit path to preserve scheduler observability (CR-001 fix); (3) full-sync defers all 8 heavy service modules (eBay, Amazon, Amazon linking, BrickLink, BrickOwl, Amazon arbitrage, Shopify, Discord) into a single parallel loadServices() call after auth passes. ACTION REQUIRED post-merge: apply the schedule change to live GCP Cloud Scheduler with `gcloud scheduler jobs update http amazon-two-phase-sync --location=europe-west2 --schedule="*/10 * * * *"` — until that's run, the GCP scheduler still fires every 5 minutes. The early-exit guard takes effect immediately on production. Smoke tests confirm all changed cron endpoints still gate on CRON_SECRET (401 without auth) and the lazy-loaded modules don't break route initialisation.
